evidence: Runtime demo, independently re-verified by the orchestrator (not just relayed from the implementing agent): `npx tsx scripts/scan-comment-citations.ts` run against the merged main branch finds ZERO instances under packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src for any of the 25 target IDs (repo total dropped from 588 to 525, consistent with the ~63 rewritten occurrences across 24 files). Direct grep of all 10 named test files for the same 25 ID patterns also returns zero matches. Full npm run build + npm run check + npm test passed (husky pre-commit gate, re-confirmed by the merge). One unplanned complication during implementation: TASK-107's own regression-pin test (scripts/scan-comment-citations.test.ts) asserted the scanner would find FEAT-006/TASK-088/etc. in the exact files TASK-108 just cleaned -- structurally guaranteed to fail post-fix, not a scanner defect. Per user correction this session (a policy-determined continuation is not a stop point), the pin was repointed at real, still-present, out-of-scope citations in packages/pi-context/src/block-api.ts (verified present via grep before use) plus a new before/after cell confirming zero pi-agent-dispatch instances remain -- 12/12 cells pass. A CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry was added to pi-agent-dispatch/CHANGELOG.md per the check-changelog gate (published-surface change); its wording itself avoids citing a literal tracker ID, since packages/pi-context/src/samples-catalog.test.ts's citation-rot-scanner gate flagged the first draft for doing exactly that -- consistent with this task's own point. Separately confirmed: issue-012's body text mentions 'types.ts' alongside work-order-loop.ts, but no types.ts file exists in pi-agent-dispatch/src -- the DEC-0047 citations actually matching that description live in packages/pi-jit-agents/src/types.ts, a different package, out of TASK-108's bounded scope and tracked as FGAP-136 follow-on, not silently closed as done.
